article_items.title: Together AI Open-Sources OSCAR: An Attention-Aware 2-Bit KV Cache Quantization System for Long-Context LLM Serving

02 SGLang installed: OSCAR is integrated into the SGLang serving framework.
The research team evaluated OSCAR on four model configurations: Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2507, Qwen3-8B, Qwen3-32B, and GLM-4.7-FP8 (358B parameters).
OSCAR integrates directly into SGLang with full paged KV-cache and prefix cache compatibility, requiring no changes to the inference client.
The research team also compared against channel-wise methods on AIME25 (Table 1).
OSCAR estimates the empirical query covariance CQ = (1/N) Σ qn ⊤ qn from a calibration set, eigen-decomposes it, and uses the eigenvectors UQ as the key rotation basis.

article_items.title: Borealis — open data, code, weights recipe for training Audio LLM
article_items.description: A Blog post by Wortega on Hugging Face
article_items.excerpt: A Blog post by Wortega on Hugging Face
article_items.summary: The mix is what the model sees at training time with noise augmentation enabled. vLLM ships a closed set of multimodal architectures out of the box ( Qwen2-Audio , LLaVA , Phi-4-MM , a couple more). The practical impact: When the user writes a prompt with one <|AUDIO|> , the processor expands it into <|start_of_audio|> + 375× <|AUDIO|> + <|start_of_audio|> , and marks each of those 375 tokens as "embedding will be supplied externally" via PromptUpdateDetails.select_token_id(..., embed_token_id=audio_token_id) . Where the 2.1× comes from. Watch next: The plugin ( vllm_borealis ) sits next to the weights in the HF model repo. Where the 2.1× comes from.
